transportation.title: Switch to Electric Vehicle
transportation.description: Electric vehicles produce 3x fewer emissions than petrol cars over their lifetime.

transportation.title: Use Public Transport
transportation.description: Buses and trains emit up to 80% less CO2 per passenger than solo car trips.

transportation.title: Cycle or Walk Short Distances
transportation.description: Zero-emission travel for trips under 5km. Also improves health.

transportation.title: Carpool with Colleagues
transportation.description: Sharing a car with 3 others cuts your transport emissions by 75%.

transportation.title: Take Train Instead of Short-Haul Flights
transportation.description: Train travel emits 6x less CO2 per km than flying. For trips under 600km, trains are often comparable in total travel time.

food.title: Adopt a Plant-Based Diet
food.description: Vegan diets produce 50-70% fewer greenhouse gas emissions than meat-heavy diets.

food.title: Reduce Beef Consumption
food.description: Beef produces 27kg CO2e per kg — the single largest dietary contributor to emissions.

food.title: Buy Local and Seasonal
food.description: Local, seasonal produce avoids air freight and long refrigerated supply chains.

food.title: Reduce Food Waste
food.description: Up to 30% of food is wasted. Wasted food accounts for 8-10% of global emissions.

energy.title: Install Solar Panels
energy.description: Solar power reduces home electricity emissions by up to 95%.

energy.title: Switch to Green Tariff
energy.description: Choose a renewable energy supplier for your home electricity.

energy.title: Improve Home Insulation
energy.description: Good insulation reduces heating/cooling energy needs by 30-50%.

energy.title: Use LED Lighting
energy.description: LED bulbs use 75% less energy than incandescent bulbs.

work.title: Work From Home
work.description: Full remote work eliminates commute emissions and reduces office energy use.

work.title: Replace Business Flights with Video Calls
work.description: A single transatlantic flight emits 1.5 tonnes CO2. Video calls produce near zero.

facts: Aviation is responsible for about 2.5% of global CO2 emissions, but its total climate impact is much higher due to contrails.
The food system accounts for about 26% of global greenhouse gas emissions.
Buildings and construction contribute 39% of global energy-related CO2 emissions.
A vegan diet uses 75% less land, produces 50% fewer greenhouse gases, and uses 55% less water than a meat diet.
The average American's carbon footprint is more than double the global average.
1 tree absorbs approximately 22kg of CO2 per year. A transatlantic flight emits ~1,600kg.
Electric vehicles are already cleaner than petrol cars in over 95% of the world's electricity grids.
If all Americans switched to a plant-based diet, it would reduce agriculture emissions by 61%.
